prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the third quarter of the Year of Our Lord 2023, the Company of Clouds and Silver Circuits did tally their treasures, and lo, the revenues rose as the dandelion seeds on a wind from the East—though no cartographer could chart the path of this sudden ascent. The stewards of the Digital Keep declared that the “net revenue retention” did soar like a monk on a jousting horse, though none in the abbey can say what it means save the bearded men in the glass-walled tower, who whisper into their glowing tablets. Behold, the "runway" was found to be sufficient for seven more moons, though whether this be a measure of food or of days is known only to the alchemists who brew the coffee at the high table. And lo, the stock did fluctuate more wildly than a goat in a cathedral, yet the High Priests of Finance did bless it with the name "growth," even as the peasants of the realm wept over their unearned shares.
